cue: Advise taking the sure thing over the bigger maybe

es.text: Más vale pájaro en mano que cien volando
es.literal: a bird in hand is worth more than a hundred flying
es.meaning: Take the certain smaller thing over the uncertain larger one.
es.example: Acepta la oferta: más vale pájaro en mano que cien volando.
es.exampleTranslation: Take the offer — a bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.

it.text: Meglio un uovo oggi che una gallina domani
it.literal: better an egg today than a hen tomorrow
it.meaning: Take the certain smaller thing now over the larger promised later.
it.example: Prendi l'anticipo: meglio un uovo oggi che una gallina domani.
it.exampleTranslation: Take the advance — better a sure thing now than a bigger maybe later.

bridge.note: Same counsel, measured on different axes. Spanish weighs QUANTITY — one bird against a hundred, all in the present. Italian weighs TIME — a small certainty today against a large promise tomorrow. The Spanish gambler risks scale; the Italian one risks patience. And Italian slyly puts the egg before the hen.

interference.es_to_it: There is no hundred-birds version in Italian; the trade is temporal — un uovo oggi, una gallina domani.
interference.it_to_es: The Spanish proverb says nothing about tomorrow; importing the time frame ('mejor un huevo hoy') loses the refrán.

review.notes: From Joe's proverb list 2026-07-18 (discovered via learner sites; all glosses and examples authored fresh). IT counterpart chosen by Claude from recall — UNVERIFIED, reviewer to confirm the pairing exists and is current. Quantity-axis vs time-axis is the payload; strong hook candidate.
